items.title: Locations released for 18 community batteries in Perth, Bunbury

children.content: Construction begins on 18 community batteries across WA, as a climate and conservation advocate calls for a legislated target for renewable energy uptake.

items.title: Discovery of woman's body in WA's Goldfields sparks homicide investigation

children.content: Police are investigating the death of a woman whose body was discovered on the outskirts of Kalgoorlie-Boulder.

image.altText: A police car parked at the side of the road with green scrub in the foreground and large trees in the background.

items.title: These ocean users have different ways of protecting themselves from sharks

children.content: After the deaths of three spearfishers from shark attacks, the conversation has turned to which shark deterrent measures are in place across the country and which ones have been found to work well.

image.altText: A man with short dark and grey hair, wearing a black t-shirt, standing in a workshop.

items.title: 'Seeking connection': The unseen children who roam the streets at night

children.content: Youth Beat workers take to Perth streets at night to help young people who have nowhere else to go.

items.title: Senior Labor figures weigh in on BHP strike, cost could be $120m per day

children.content: Federal Resources Minister Madeleine King says workers threatening strike action at the nation's biggest port deserve "every single cent" of what they earn while a peak industry body says they are "letting down everyone".

image.altText: Upwards shot of the BHP building in Perth.

items.title: Expert medical report on Floreat murderer sought ahead of inquest

children.content: The main focus of a coronial inquest into the death of a Perth mother and her daughter will be the medical care their killer Mark Bombara received in the lead up to the double-murder suicide, a coroner reveals.

image.altText: Jennifer and Gretl Petelczyc who were shot dead in their own home by Mark James Bombara.

items.title: Why the swimming pool is an oasis in these outback communities

children.content: In Kalumburu and Balgo, two of Western Australia's most isolated towns, investing in a community swimming pool has been a boon for everyone.

items.title: Andrew Forrest defends native title deals after $150m Yindjibarndi court loss

children.content: Mining magnate Andrew "Twiggy" Forrest has defended his company's approach to Indigenous agreements after a court ordered the miner to pay $150 million in compensation to the Yindjibarndi people.

children.content: Port Adelaide leads Sydney after a strong first half at the Adelaide Oval as North Melbourne escapes with a win against West Coast. Follow live.

image.altText: Isaac Heeney handballs while being tackled

items.title: Killers of Greenbushes man show no remorse during sentencing

children.content: A judge said the men, who were convicted of killing 68-year-old Raymond Smith during a drug-fuelled home invasion in the WA town of Greenbushes, showed no remorse.

image.altText: An older man with a long pony tail pats a big dog.

items.title: Quick hits: Legends stunned by on-air gaffe, Trump's call goes cold

children.content: The US and Canada hit the pitch for the first time at their home World Cup, but on day two the drama extended from the field to the TV booths and beyond. Here are the quick hits.

articles.title: Underdog status embraced as Socceroos prepare for Türkiye

children.content: Australia's upcoming opponents, Türkiye, have generational talent and are keen to end generational heartache.

image.altText: Footballer jogging during a training session, holding his thumb up

articles.title: US romps to 4-1 victory over Paraguay in electric World Cup opener

children.content: World Cup co-host USA gets its campaign underway with a dominant 4-1 thrashing of Paraguay after Canada rescued a late draw with Bosnia and Herzegovina in Toronto.

image.altText: Chris Richards leaps on Folarin Balogun's back in celebration of a goal

articles.title: 'A footballer's dream': From humble beginnings to the world stage

children.content: The Touré brothers snuck into their local football field after hours to play the game they loved, while the Yengi brothers kicked around makeshift balls made of plastic bags. Two of them have now made it onto the world stage as Socceroos.

image.altText: composite of a Mo Toure as a young boy in a soccer field and him as an adult in a Socceroos guernsey

items.title: New line of hope in discovering the Nannup Four

children.content: The vice-president of a search team that solved two high-profile missing persons cases in Tasmania turns his attention to the Nannup Four case after it was featured in the Expanse podcast.

image.altText: A photo of two men, a woman and a girl with a photo of bushland in the background.

items.title: After 70 bags of fruit were stolen in one weekend, farmer closes roadside stall

children.content: Mandarin grower Bruce Crain says people's growing dishonesty, including the theft of 70 bags in one weekend, makes it impossible to offer a roadside service.

image.altText: Several bags of mandarins on a black table with a sign reading "$5 per bag" placed inside of a corrugated steel shelter.

items.title: Pauline Hanson reveals Gina Rinehart's role in One Nation policy making

children.content: Pauline Hanson speaks of her "respect" for billionaire Gina Rinehart, becoming emotional during a speech in Perth recalling her time in detention.

items.title: What Australia would have looked like when the dinosaurs roamed

children.content: Palaeontologists say evidence pointing to Australia's polar past shows that many dinosaurs roamed the country when the south east was still within the Antarctic Circle.

items.title: Britain's defence strategy under fire while global nuclear spending surges

children.content: The prospect of nuclear weapons being used in either Ukraine or the Middle East, rather than being fired between Moscow, Washington or Beijing has risen.

items.title: Beachside towns in WA face difficult decisions as erosion gets worse

children.content: A week after Western Australia experienced one of its worst storms in decades, some stretches of coast are crumbling into the ocean at an alarming rate. Millions of dollars' worth of infrastructure is at risk heading into winter.

items.title: Massive whale graveyard found in deep ocean off Australia

children.content: Hundreds of whale fossils, including those from extinct species new to science, range in age from recent to 5 million years old and are also home to undescribed deep-sea creatures.
